pattern: A tree only offers downward edges, so reaching nodes that are 'behind' the target requires first turning it into an undirected graph via parent pointers, then a plain breadth-first search out to radius k.
hints: A recursive walk only reaches descendants; you also need a way to step from a node back to its parent.
Do one pass to record each node's parent, then treat left child, right child and parent as three equal neighbors.
Breadth-first search outward from the target for exactly k layers, marking visited nodes so you never step back the way you came.

edge_inputs.description: target in the middle of the tree, answer spans both subtrees and the parent side
single-node tree, k larger than any possible distance
k = 0 returns just the target itself
target is the root, distance measured purely downward
target at a leaf; k walks back up past the root without an index error
straight chain (no branching) so the BFS must not revisit the node it came from
value 0 present at the target itself, guards against falsy-value bugs

stress.description: random tree shape with a random target node and radius
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 200))
    vals = rng.sample(range(0, 501), n)
    def build(items):
        if not items:
            return None
        if len(items) == 1:
            return [items[0], None, None]
        i = rng.randrange(len(items))
        root_val = items[i]
        rest = items[:i] + items[i + 1:]
        rng.shuffle(rest)
        split = rng.randint(0, len(rest))
        return [root_val, build(rest[:split]), build(rest[split:])]
    tree = build(vals)
    out = []
    queue = [tree]
    while queue:
        node = queue.pop(0)
        if node is None:
            out.append(None)
            continue
        out.append(node[0])
        queue.append(node[1])
        queue.append(node[2])
    while out and out[-1] is None:
        out.pop()
    target = rng.choice(vals)
    k = rng.randint(0, 10)
    return [out, target, k]
